name: veritas-default
description: Conservative starter policy pack for a newly bootstrapped AI-guided repository.

rules.message: The tracked Veritas operational artifacts must stay present so agents and reviewers share the same baseline.

explain.summary: Keep the repo-local Veritas governance baseline present.
mustDo: Restore missing .veritas operational files before finishing.
mustNotDo: Do not delete or weaken governance files to make a run pass.
explain.exampleGood: Update the schema and default policy pack together.

rules.message: The shipped Veritas CLI entrypoints and package manifest must stay present.

explain.summary: Keep the CLI and package entrypoints available.
mustDo: Preserve package metadata and shipped bin files.
mustNotDo: Do not move CLI entrypoints without updating package.json.
explain.exampleGood: Add a new subcommand in bin/veritas.mjs and export its runner.
explain.exampleBad: Delete bin/veritas-report.mjs while leaving it in package.json.

rules.message: The repo must keep the docs that explain the shipped Veritas surface.

explain.summary: Keep reference docs aligned with framework behavior.
mustDo: Update docs when schemas, commands, or artifact shapes change.
mustNotDo: Do not document behavior that is not wired.
explain.exampleGood: Document a new rule kind in artifacts-and-schemas.md.
explain.exampleBad: Claim a command exists before bin/veritas.mjs dispatches it.

rules.message: The shipped Veritas schema set must stay present and reviewable.

explain.summary: Schemas are the contract for adapters, policy packs, evidence, and evals.
mustDo: Update the relevant schema with any new persisted field.
mustNotDo: Do not add artifact fields that schemas reject.
explain.exampleGood: Add a rule kind to schema and evaluator registry together.
explain.exampleBad: Allow additionalProperties for unknown rule match shapes.

rules.message: All required AI tool instruction files must contain the Veritas governance block.

explain.summary: AI instruction files must carry the canonical governance block.
mustDo: Regenerate or reapply the governance block after edits.
mustNotDo: Do not hand-edit the marker-bounded block into a different meaning.
explain.exampleGood: Run veritas apply governance-blocks after changing instructions.
explain.exampleBad: Remove the governance block from CLAUDE.md.

rules.message: Prefer keeping the tracked Veritas proof and check-in integration surfaces present so changes flow through the documented operational path before review.

explain.summary: Keep hook and check-in integration artifacts in place.
mustDo: Preserve the local hook and workflow paths or update this rule deliberately.
mustNotDo: Do not bypass check-in integration by deleting hook artifacts.
explain.exampleGood: Update both hook builder and default policy when adding a hook.

rules.message: Source files should not leave console.log diagnostics behind.

explain.summary: Remove ad hoc console.log calls from source files.
mustDo: Use structured reporting or tests instead of committed console.log calls.
mustNotDo: Do not leave temporary debugging output in src/**.
explain.exampleGood: Return explicit feedback from a CLI command.
